methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $141M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
MacroFab, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $141M to date, is valued against a peer set of nine same-sector companies at comparable or adjacent stages. The universe includes eight private firms—among them Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), and Exotec ($2.0B Series D)—plus one public comparable.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $141M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $240M. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company comp set yields $1.6B, with the peer range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This wide dispersion reflects stage heterogeneity and company-specific risk, but the median anchors the upper boundary of a reasonable valuation band.

Triangulating these two methods produces a range of $204M to $1.8B, with a median of $920M. The gap between stage-progression ($240M) and peer-median ($1.6B) reflects MacroFab's earlier stage relative to several comparables and uncertainty around revenue scale, unit economics, and market position within the sector. Key sensitivities include go-to-market traction, gross margins, and whether subsequent-stage funding multiples compress or expand given broader venture market conditions. A defensible valuation range would cluster toward the lower half of the peer band unless operational metrics materially exceed peer benchmarks.
